LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT TOOK DEFENDANT HANS FLEISCHNER'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER TRANSFERRING THIS ACTION THE PROPER COURT; REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IN THE FORM OF REASONABLE EXPENSES AND ATTORNEY'S FEES INCURRED IN MAKING THIS MOTION TO TRANSFER UNDER SUBMISSION. JUDGE: LESLIE C. NICHOLS; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185 *AFTER HEARING, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT HANS FLEISCHNER'S NOTICE OF MOTION FOR AN ORDER TRANSFERRING THIS ACTION THE PROPER COURT AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IN THE FORM OF REASONABLE EXPENSES AND ATTORNEY'S FEES INCURRED IN MAKING THIS MOTION TO TRANSFER IS GRANTED. CCP 395(A) APPLIES BECAUSE THE CAUSES OF ACTION ARISE FROM A CONTRACTUAL RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN THE PARTIES. UNDER CCP 395(A), VENUE IS PROPER IN THE COUNTY: (1) WHERE THE OBLIGATION IS TO BE PERFORMED, (2) WHERE THE CONTRACT WAS ENTERED INTO, AND (3) WHERE THE DEFENDANT RESIDES. CCP 395(A) FURTHER PROVIDES THAT THE COUNTY WHERE THE OBLIGATION IS INCURRED IS THE COUNTY WHERE IT IS TO BE PERFORMED, UNLESS THERE IS A SPECIAL CONTRACT IN WRITING TO THE CONTRARY. THE CONTRACT WAS ENTERED INTO IN RIVERSIDE COUNTY AND THERE IS NO EVIDENCE OF A SPECIAL CONTRACT IN WRITING TO THE CONTRARY. DEFENDANT RESIDES IN RIVERSIDE COUNTY. THEREFORE, VENUE IS PROPER IN RIVERSIDE COUNTY. PLAINTIFFS ARE ORDERED TO PAY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,375 TO DEFENDANT PER CCP 396B(B). WRITTEN ORDER SIGNED AFTER HEARING.*
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF JUN-26-2013 CONTINUED TO AUG-14-2013 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-03-2013 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-24-2013 Continued To Dec-18-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion For An Order Transferring This Action The Proper Court; Notice Of Request For Sancitons In The Form Of Reasonable Expenses And Attorney'S Fees Incurred In Making This Motion To Transfer
Off Calendar Oct-10-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: WILLIAM GWIRE, ESQ., 415.296.8880 X227, FOR PLAINTIFF EDWARD STOLZ; No other appearances.

RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT TOOK DEFENDANT HANS FLEISCHNER'S Motion For An Order Transferring This Action The Proper Court; Request For SanCTIONS In The Form Of Reasonable Expenses And Attorney's Fees Incurred In Making This Motion To Transfer UNDER SUBMISSION. Judge: Leslie C. Nichols; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185
*AFTER HEARING, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT HANS FLEISCHNER'S Notice Of Motion For An Order Transferring This Action The Proper Court AND Request For SanCTIons In The Form Of Reasonable Expenses And Attorney's Fees Incurred In Making This Motion To Transfer IS Granted. CCP 395(a) applies because the causes of action arise from a contractual relationship between the parties. Under CCP 395(a), venue is proper in the county: (1) where the obligation is to be performed, (2) where the contract was entered into, and (3) where the defendant resides. CCP 395(a) further provides that the county where the obligation is incurred is the county where it is to be performed, unless there is a special contract in writing to the contrary. The contract was entered into in Riverside County and there is no evidence of a special contract in writing to the contrary. Defendant resides in Riverside County. Therefore, venue is proper in Riverside County. Plaintiffs are ordered to pay sanctions in the amount of $2,375 to defendant per CCP 396b(b). WRITTEN ORDER SIGNED AFTER HEARING.*

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-05-2013 Continued To Oct-29-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion For An Order Transferring This Action The Proper Court; Notice Of Request For Sancitons In The Form Of Reasonable Expenses And Attorney'S Fees Incurred In Making This Motion To Transfer
Off Calendar Sep-20-2013 Continued To Oct-10-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT HANS FLEISCHNER'S Motion For An Order Transferring This Action The Proper Court; Notice Of Request For Sancitons In The Form Of Reasonable Expenses And Attorney'S Fees Incurred In Making This Motion To Transfer is Continued to October 10, 2013 at 9:30 a.m. On September 17, 2013, Counsel for plaintiff filed a declaration and request for continuance of the hearing on this motion on the ground that he had not received a copy of the moving papers, apparently due to the fact that it was sent to his old office address. Opposition and reply briefs may be filed in accordance with CCP 1005. Courtesy copies must be delivered to Department 302 on the day of filing. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/mjm
